title: marsdenia eriocaulis
intro: Marsdenia eriocaulis, also known as the "Yellow Vine," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in India and Sri Lanka. It is a member of the Asclepiadaceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Marsdenia eriocaulis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts for its medicinal purposes.
